headline: HOGS EXPLODE 3.7% AS PORK RALLY IGNITES
subheadline: Lean hogs surge to $103.35 while grains grind higher on China trade optimism.
lead: Lean hogs exploded 3.7% to $103.35, the biggest single-session move in six weeks, as the pork complex finally caught fire after months of sideways drift. The overnight surge came with no specific catalyst beyond what looks like short-covering panic in thin Monday morning trade. Grains tagged along for modest gains, corn up a quarter to $4.70 as China's $17 billion annual ag purchase commitment through 2028 adds fresh buying interest to the fundamental story.
the_takeaway: Hogs lead the protein complex higher, grains follow on China optimism.
teaser: Hogs surge 3.7% in overnight explosion, corn climbs on China trade momentum.

one_number.unit: China's annual US ag purchase commitment through 2028
one_number.context: The White House announced China will buy at least $17 billion in US agricultural products annually through 2028, beyond existing soybean commitments. That's real money flowing into the grain complex just as planting season peaks and the funds have been net short. The commitment spans beans, beef, and poultry, giving the livestock rally fresh fundamental legs.

sections.title: Hogs Lead Protein Surge
sections.body: Lean hogs exploded 3.7% to $103.35 in the overnight session, the biggest single-day move since early April. The surge came with **no specific news catalyst**, suggesting short-covering panic in thin holiday-adjacent trade. Feeders joined the party, up 0.9% to $361.45, while live cattle gained a more modest 0.6% to $247.93. The protein complex has been coiled for weeks after the grain crash failed to drag livestock down with it.
sections.catalyst: No clean catalyst, looks like short-covering in thin overnight trade
sections.bottom_line: Protein complex finally breaks higher after weeks of consolidation.

sections.title: Grains Rise on China Deal
sections.body: Corn climbed a quarter to $4.70 as China's $17 billion annual ag purchase commitment gave the grain complex fresh fundamental support. Soybeans gained 3.5¢ to $12.01, with the November contract up 2.25¢ to $11.95, as the carry trade continues working in beans. Chicago wheat added 1.75¢ to $6.55. The moves are modest but **directionally important**: the funds have been net short grains for two weeks, and China buying interest changes the supply-demand calculus heading into prevent plant season.
sections.catalyst: China commits to $17B annual US ag purchases through 2028
sections.bottom_line: China deal adds buying interest just as funds turn net short.

sections.title: Energy Slides on Demand Fears
sections.body: Crude fell 1.1% to $102.07 as Chinese refiners slashed crude runs to the lowest level since August 2022, cutting demand despite ongoing Middle East supply concerns. Natural gas bucked the trend, gaining 1.0% to $3.05 on weekend storage data showing draws above the five-year average. The **crude-natgas divergence** reflects different demand dynamics: China's refinery cuts are demand destruction, while US gas storage remains tight heading into summer cooling season.
sections.catalyst: Chinese refiners cut crude runs to lowest since Aug 2022
sections.bottom_line: Crude demand concerns outweigh supply risk for now.

outside_the_pit.title: Missouri farmers resume planting after weeks of rain delays
outside_the_pit.body: Missouri producers finally got back in the field after multiple weeks of cool, wet conditions left some fields too saturated for equipment. Some areas so wet they need replanting, which tightens the window for prevent plant decisions across the central Belt.

outside_the_pit.title: South Africa FMD crisis escalates as cattle reinfected post-vaccination
outside_the_pit.body: Vaccinated cattle in South Africa are becoming reinfected with foot-and-mouth disease, raising questions about vaccine efficacy and putting global beef trade protocols under scrutiny. The outbreak highlights disease risk premiums in protein markets.

outside_the_pit.title: Colorado River gets $47M federal funding for crisis management
outside_the_pit.body: Four Colorado projects receive federal funding to address ongoing water shortages in the Colorado River system. Western water scarcity continues reshaping crop selection and acreage decisions from Arizona to Wyoming.

spread_to_watch.label: July corn / December corn calendar spread
spread_to_watch.commentary: The Dec-July spread is holding 24 cents of carry as old crop supply stays tight and new crop weather premium starts building. When this carry starts narrowing, it signals either old crop loosening up or new crop getting too cheap. Right now it's pricing normal seasonal patterns, no stress signals yet.

basis.headline: Central Belt basis firms on China optimism, Gulf steady
basis.body: **Corn basis is talking** in the central Belt as the China purchase commitment adds fresh demand to an already tight old-crop situation. Eastern elevators firming bids by 5-8 cents over the weekend. Gulf basis holding steady as export pace remains strong but not accelerating. Western Belt still soft, consistent with harvest pressure timing.

weekly_thread.question: Will the China trade momentum carry through to Thursday's export sales?
weekly_thread.status_text: China's $17 billion annual commitment gives grain bulls fresh ammunition, but the real test is whether weekly export sales start reflecting actual purchases versus promises. Thursday's data will show if the optimism is backed by boats.

the_more_you_know.title: Why overnight moves stick (and when they don't)
the_more_you_know.body: Hogs' 3.7% overnight surge happened in thin trade with no specific catalyst, classic short-covering panic. These moves stick when they align with fundamentals (tight pork supplies, China buying interest) and fail when they're purely technical. The key tell: does the cash market follow? If pork cutouts start climbing this week, the futures move was real. If not, expect the gains to fade by Wednesday.

watch_list.desc: USDA Crop Progress: corn planting above 85% removes weather premium
Weekly export sales: soy sales above 500K MT confirms China momentum
Pork cutout values: need to confirm futures surge with cash strength

daily_quote.text: Optimism is the faith that leads to achievement. Nothing can be done without hope and confidence.

source_summary: USDA, CME, EIA, White House trade announcement, industry reports
